text: In this video we demonstrate our technique of right hemohepatectomy using the three-device dissection technique for parenchymal dissection in robotic assisted liver surgery.
The patient suffered from liver metastasis in the right liver from rectal carcinoma.
As prior surgery, rectum amputation with the syndostoma and open cholecystectomy was performed.
The surgeon operates using the da Vinci XI robotic system, which is visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon mobilizes the liver from the diaphragm, revealing the supra-hepatic inferior vena cava (IVC).
In firefly mode, the surgeon identifies the ICG-stained liver metastasis, which appears as a fluorescent area within the liver tissue.
The surgeon mobilizes adhesions between the liver and the right colon and transverse colon, separating these structures that were attached due to prior surgery.
The surgeon places a Pringle maneuver around the hepatoduodenal ligament, temporarily occluding blood flow to the liver.
The surgeon uses intraoperative ultrasound to screen the liver, visually identifying any additional liver metastases.
The surgeon marks the resection margin on the liver surface, making an incision through the liver capsule to delineate the area for resection.
The surgeon identifies the right hepatic artery, applies a clip to occlude it, and then cuts the artery, completing the vascular control step.
The surgeon loops the right hepatic branch of the portal vein with a suture, then clips and cuts the vessel, completing the vascular isolation.
The surgeon mobilizes the liver from the inferior vena cava (IVC), carefully separating the liver tissue from the surrounding structures.
The surgeon selectively clips and cuts the Spigellian veins, isolating them from surrounding tissue within the liver.
The surgeon performs liver parenchymal dissection using a three-device technique, with the assistant utilizing a laparoscopic aquachet to aid in the process.
The robotic surgeon performs liver parenchymal dissection using bipolar Maryland forceps and monopolar scissors, carefully manipulating the tissue.
The surgeon identifies the right bile duct intrahepatically, then clips and cuts it using surgical instruments.
The surgeon identifies the right hepatic vein and divides it using a surgical stapler, completing the separation of this vessel from surrounding tissue.
The surgeon packs the liver in a retrieval pack and removes it through the scar from the previous cholecystectomy procedure.
Thanks for watching.
The patient suffered from liver metastasis in the right liver from rectal carcinoma.
